text:
6: humans don't use channels switch -- that's only for agents [...] your first example looks wrong: channels are always scoped to the current project, if the project is "tenex-edge" then swithich to the tenex-edge/planning channel would just be "planning" (otherwise everything would require the tenex-edge prefix like tenex-edge/epic999/planning)

if agents want to indicate a channel of a different project maybe we can give them something like tenex-edge channel read --project some-other-project epic2344/research -- (switching channels to a different project is probably a bad idea, or at least not necessary now)
 [...] $ tenex-edge channels switch planning
ERROR ambiguous_channel: 'planning' matched 2. Re-run with an exact path:
  tenex-edge channels switch planning
  tenex-edge channels switch epic999/planning
exit: 2
```
(Both candidates are within `tenex-edge`; no project prefix appears. The escape hatch `@3f9a` still applies for two siblings sharing a name at the same level.) [...] - `name` — relative: direct children → deeper descendants within the current project
- `parent/child` — relative path within the current project (suffix-matchable)
- `@3f9a` — opaque-id escape hatch for same-level name collisions
- ~~`/root/child` absolute~~ — **removed** (there's no cross-project switch)
- cross-project access only via explicit `--project <slug>` on read-type commands
